description: In addDaytonaLocked() (manager.go:926), a branch is created via repoGit.CreateBranchFrom(branchName, startPoint). For local polecats, this branch is implicitly cleaned up when the worktree is removed. For remote polecats, RemoveWithOptions only removes the marker directory — the branch in .repo.git is never deleted. Over time, this leads to accumulation of orphaned branches. Fix: In RemoveWithOptions, after worktree removal attempts, explicitly delete the polecat's branch from .repo.git for remote polecats (or for all polecats as a safety measure).
notes: Implemented: Added cleanupPolecatBranches() that deletes polecat branches from .repo.git during removal. Reads branch name from agent bead before reset, then also sweeps for orphan branches matching polecat/<name>/* and polecat/<name>-* patterns. Applied to both remote (removeDaytonaWorkspace) and local (worktree) removal paths.
title: Remote polecat branches never cleaned up in .repo.git

description: HIGH: cleanupOrphanPolecatState() in manager.go:~2170 scans polecat directories for a .git subdirectory. Remote (daytona) polecats have marker dirs with NO git worktree inside them. The function classifies these as orphaned and calls os.RemoveAll(polecatDir). This runs during normal pool reconciliation via reconcilePoolInternal(), silently destroying all remote polecat markers. This breaks List(), exists() checks, and later removeDaytonaWorkspace() calls. Fix: Make cleanupOrphanPolecatState daytona-aware — check for a corresponding agent bead with DaytonaWorkspace set before deleting, or check for a marker file that distinguishes remote polecat dirs.
notes: Fix: Added isRemoteMode() check in cleanupOrphanPolecatState to skip remote polecat marker directories. Remote polecats have marker dirs without git worktrees — their cleanup is handled by Remove/removeDaytonaWorkspace. Added two regression tests: one verifying remote markers are preserved, one verifying local orphan cleanup still works.
title: cleanupOrphanPolecatState deletes remote polecat marker directories

description: HIGH: getPolecatManager() in cmd/polecat.go creates a Manager without calling SetDaytona(), so daytonaClient is nil. In RemoveWithOptions, when it reaches a remote polecat (daytonaWS \!= '' from agent bead), removeDaytonaWorkspace() guards with 'if m.daytonaClient \!= nil' and silently skips the workspace stop/delete. The marker directory and name pool are cleaned up, but the Daytona workspace continues running indefinitely. Every CLI-initiated polecat removal of a remote polecat leaks a running Daytona workspace. Fix: Load rig settings in getPolecatManager, check for RemoteBackend, create daytona client and call SetDaytona() when present.
notes: Fix: getPolecatManager() now loads rig settings and initializes daytona client when RemoteBackend is configured. This ensures all CLI removal paths (nuke, remove, stale --cleanup, gc) properly call daytona stop/delete instead of silently skipping. Added 3 tests for removeDaytonaWorkspace covering nil client, with client (stop), and AutoDelete (delete).
title: CLI getPolecatManager() missing SetDaytona — nuke leaks remote workspaces

Added gatherPolecatAgentBeads(), reportToDiscoverResult(), and buildReconcileActions() adapter functions. All existing tests pass. JSON output format preserved.

description: MEDIUM: reconcile.go only checks State \!= 'stopped' before calling daytona stop on orphaned workspaces. Workspaces in 'creating', 'starting', 'stopping', or 'error' states may fail or behave unpredictably when stopped. Similarly, restartDaytonaPolecatSession only handles 'stopped' explicitly, proceeding to exec into workspaces in other states. Fix: Add state-aware handling — skip 'creating'/'stopping' (transient), handle 'error' specially, only stop 'running' workspaces.
notes: Implemented: Added state-aware handling in Reconcile() for orphaned workspaces (skip creating/starting/stopping, handle error distinctly, only stop running). Added state-aware switch in restartDaytonaPolecatSession() that returns clear errors for non-ready states. Added 4 new tests covering transitional states, error state, and auto-delete interactions.
title: Reconcile doesn't handle transitional workspace states (creating/error)

description: Two daemon timing issues: (1) daemon.go:2105-2141: restartDaytonaPolecatSession uses a single context with DaytonaCreateTimeout (120s) for both ListOwned AND Start. If ListOwned takes 30s, Start only gets 90s. Fix: use independent timeouts. (2) manager.go:1545,983: removeDaytonaWorkspace and cleanupOnError use DaytonaListTimeout (30s) for Stop/Delete operations. These are heavier than list and routinely take longer under load, causing spurious timeouts and orphaned workspaces. Fix: define DaytonaStopTimeout (60s) or use DaytonaCreateTimeout.
notes: Implemented: Added DaytonaStopTimeout (60s) constant. Split daemon.go restartDaytonaPolecatSession shared context into independent timeouts for ListOwned (30s) and Start (120s). Changed manager.go removeDaytonaWorkspace and cleanupOnError from DaytonaListTimeout to DaytonaStopTimeout for Stop/Delete ops.
title: Fix daemon restart shared context budget and Stop/Delete timeout

In polecat_discover.go, the same short-lived context (DaytonaListTimeout) is used for both ListOwned and the subsequent reconciliation. If listing consumes most of the 30s budget, stop/archive/delete actions can be prematurely canceled even if their per-operation timeout is larger (child contexts can't extend the parent deadline). Fix: use separate contexts — keep a short timeout for listing, and create a fresh (or longer) context for reconcile operations. Affects lines ~142 and ~180 in internal/cmd/polecat_discover.go. From Copilot review on PR #2438.
title: polecat_discover: separate contexts for list vs reconcile operations

description: Two client-level fixes: (1) retry.go:125-129: Jitter formula only ADDS to delay (range [delay, delay+delay*Jitter]). Standard jitter should be +/-Jitter/2 to center around configured value. As-is with Jitter=0.25, delays are always 100-125% of base, biasing high and defeating thundering-herd mitigation. Fix: delta = (rand.Float64() - 0.5) * jitterRange. (2) client.go:218-221: ListOwned does json.Unmarshal on stdout which fails with unexpected end of JSON input when daytona returns empty string. Fix: treat empty/whitespace stdout as empty list before attempting unmarshal.
notes: Implemented both fixes: (1) retry.go:127 - centered jitter with (rand-0.5)*range so delays are symmetric around base delay, (2) client.go:218-221 - empty/whitespace stdout from daytona list returns empty slice instead of JSON parse error. Tests updated and all passing.
title: Fix retry jitter bias and ListOwned empty JSON handling

description: HIGH: reconcileDaytonaWorkspaces runs at daemon startup and snapshots all workspaces via ListOwned. If gt sling is concurrently spawning a new polecat: (1) ListOwned doesn't include the new workspace yet, (2) sling creates workspace + agent bead, (3) daemon finds bead with daytona_workspace but no matching workspace in snapshot, (4) daemon classifies bead as orphaned and clears daytona_workspace. The just-spawned polecat loses its workspace reference. Fix: Add a grace period (skip beads younger than N minutes) or use a lock/flag to signal active spawns.
notes: Implemented: skip beads with AgentState=spawning in DiscoverWorkspaces to prevent race with concurrent gt sling. Added SpawningSkipped counter for observability. All tests pass.
title: Daemon reconcile vs concurrent spawn race condition

description: MEDIUM: ShortInstallationID() in config/types.go truncates the UUID to 8 hex chars (32 bits entropy). Birthday paradox gives ~1%% collision at 9,300 installations, ~50%% at 77,000. Fix: Increase to 12+ characters and centralize the prefix construction (currently 'gt-' + shortID assembled in 4+ places).
notes: Implemented: Increased ShortInstallationID from 8 to 12 chars (48 bits entropy). Removed inline duplication in daemon.go. Updated all comments and tests.
title: 8-character short installation ID has birthday collision risk

description: HIGH/SECURITY: writeFileInWorkspace() in manager.go interpolates the path parameter directly into a shell command: fmt.Sprintf("echo '%%s' | base64 -d > %%s", encoded, path). Currently safe because paths are hardcoded (/run/gt-proxy/client.crt etc), but the function signature accepts arbitrary paths. A future caller passing user-controlled input enables command injection inside the workspace container. Fix: Use shellescape or pass path as a separate argument to avoid shell interpretation. E.g., use daytona exec with explicit args instead of sh -c.
notes: Fixed: writeFileInWorkspace now passes path and encoded data as shell positional args ($1, $2) via 'sh -c script _ arg1 arg2' instead of interpolating them into fmt.Sprintf. Added regression test TestWriteFileInWorkspace_NoShellInjection that verifies malicious paths are not present in the shell script string.
title: Shell injection possible in writeFileInWorkspace path parameter

description: HIGH: addDaytonaLocked holds the per-polecat file lock through all Daytona API calls: workspace create (120s timeout), multiple exec calls for cert injection and gt prime. Total potential hold time >2 minutes. If the process is killed mid-operation (OOM, SIGKILL), the file lock goes stale and cleanupOnError never runs, leaving orphaned resources. Fix: Consider releasing the polecat lock before network calls and re-acquiring after, or use a timeout-based lock with automatic staleness detection.
notes: Implemented: Released polecat lock after fast local operations (steps 1-3: branch, cert, agent bead) but before slow Daytona network calls (step 4: workspace create, cert injection, gt prime). Renamed addDaytonaLocked -> addDaytona since it now manages its own lock lifecycle. All tests pass.
title: Polecat lock held during 2+ min network calls in addDaytonaLocked

design:
# Code Review: Daytona Polecat Lifecycle Implementation

## Summary
Thorough review of all 14 dependency beads (Steps 1.1–6.3) against design doc at docs/design/sandboxed-polecat-implementation-plan.md. Filed 8 beads for issues found.

## Overall Assessment
Implementation is **solid and well-structured** with good separation of concerns. Phase 1 (config), Phase 2 (client), and Phase 6 (CLI) are excellent. Phase 3 (lifecycle) has two critical bugs. Phase 4-5 have moderate gaps.

## Critical Issues (P1)
1. **gtd-d2d**: Remote polecat cert serial never stored. addDaytonaLocked() uses proxyCA.IssuePolecat() which doesn't return a serial. denyCertForPolecat() finds CertSerial=="" and skips revocation. mTLS certs for remote polecats are NEVER revoked.
2. **gtd-fx2**: RemoveWithOptions never deletes daytona workspace. The daytona workspace continues running after polecat removal, consuming cloud resources indefinitely.

## Important Issues (P2)
3. **gtd-0ly**: Remote branches in .repo.git never cleaned up during removal.
4. **gtd-xah**: No RemoteBackend provider validation at config load time.
5. **gtd-snh**: Proxy admin address hardcoded to 127.0.0.1:9877 in preflight check.
6. **gtd-988**: polecat discover --reconcile has no dry-run mode despite underlying support.
7. **gtd-fbl**: Missing tests for cert lifecycle, daemon reconciliation, and daytona restart paths.

## Minor Issues (P3)
8. **gtd-z1n**: ParseWorkspaceName assumes polecat names have no hyphens (undocumented constraint).

## Phase-by-Phase Assessment

### Phase 1 (Foundation): PASS - 95%
- InstallationID: correct UUID v4, auto-gen, persistence, ShortInstallationID helper ✓
- RemoteBackend struct: all 7 fields match spec, pointer on RigSettings ✓
- DaytonaWorkspaceID: correctly added to Polecat struct ✓
- Tests: good coverage for InstallationID, missing RemoteBackend roundtrip test

### Phase 2 (Client): PASS - 98%
- All lifecycle methods implemented (Create, Start, Stop, Delete, Exec) ✓
- ListOwned with installPrefix filtering ✓
- WorkspaceName/ParseWorkspaceName with edge case handling ✓
- Command execution safe (exec.CommandContext, no shell injection) ✓
- 29+ tests with quality mocks ✓
- Only gap: ParseWorkspaceName hyphen assumption (gtd-z1n)

### Phase 3 (Lifecycle): PARTIAL - 75%
- Remote mode detection correct everywhere ✓
- Marker directories created correctly ✓
- Worktree creation properly skipped ✓
- daytona exec command built correctly with env vars ✓
- GT_PROCESS_NAMES updated for liveness detection ✓
- AutoStop on session stop works ✓
- CRITICAL: Cert serial not stored for remote path (gtd-d2d)
- CRITICAL: RemoveWithOptions missing workspace cleanup (gtd-fx2)
- Branch cleanup missing on removal (gtd-0ly)

### Phase 4 (Discovery): PASS - 90%
- Reconciliation logic correct with good test coverage ✓
- Heartbeat patrol correctly restarts daytona polecats ✓
- Workspace state checked before restart ✓
- Gap: No daemon-level tests for reconciliation

### Phase 5 (Proxy): PARTIAL - 80%
- Cert issuance works but serial not stored (blocks revocation)
- Cert injection via base64 into container works
- Preflight checks verify CLI, proxy, CA ✓
- Gap: Hardcoded admin address

### Phase 6 (CLI): PASS - 90%
- --daytona flag with auto-detect from rig config ✓
- polecat list shows BACKEND column ✓
- polecat discover with --reconcile ✓
- Gap: No --dry-run exposed despite package support
notes: Code review complete. Filed 8 issues: 2 critical (P1), 5 important (P2), 1 minor (P3). Critical: cert revocation broken for remote polecats (gtd-d2d), workspace cleanup missing in RemoveWithOptions (gtd-fx2). See --design field for full breakdown.
title: Code review: Daytona polecat lifecycle implementation

description: In addDaytonaLocked() (manager.go:934), m.proxyCA.IssuePolecat() is called directly which returns certPEM/keyPEM but NOT a serial number. The local path (manager.go:829) uses m.issueCertForPolecat() which calls proxyAdmin.IssueCert(), gets a serial, and stores it via UpdateAgentCertSerial (manager.go:1507). But the remote/daytona path never stores a cert serial in the agent bead. When denyCertForPolecat() runs during removal (manager.go:1525), it finds fields.CertSerial == '' and returns early without revoking the cert. Result: remote polecat mTLS certificates are issued but NEVER revoked. After a remote polecat is removed, its certificate remains valid indefinitely. Fix: Remote path should use proxyAdmin.IssueCert() (same as local path) or extract serial from the issued cert and store it via UpdateAgentCertSerial().
notes: Fixed: addDaytonaLocked now parses the cert PEM returned by proxyCA.IssuePolecat() to extract the serial number (using x509.ParseCertificate + SerialNumber.Text(16)) and stores it in the agent bead via UpdateAgentCertSerial. This ensures denyCertForPolecat can revoke the cert during polecat removal, matching the local path behavior.
title: Remote polecat cert serial never stored — certs never revoked

description: HIGH: In addDaytonaLocked() manager.go:1005-1014, failed writeFileInWorkspace for cert/key/CA files is only a PrintWarning. The polecat is returned as successfully created but cannot authenticate to the proxy — it's a non-functional workspace that will fail at the first git operation with a cryptic mTLS error. Similarly, gt prime failure (line 1019-1022) is non-fatal. Fix: Make cert file injection failures a hard error that triggers cleanupOnError and returns an error. The polecat should not be created if it can't communicate with the proxy.
notes: Fix: Changed cert injection (writeFileInWorkspace for client.crt, client.key, ca.crt) and gt prime failures from PrintWarning to hard errors with cleanupOnError + return error in addDaytonaLocked(). Prevents creation of non-functional polecats that can't authenticate to the proxy.
title: Cert injection failure should be hard error, not warning

description: Two nil/empty guards missing: (1) reconcile.go:186-275: Reconcile() only nil-checks logger on the ActionHealthy path (line 281). All orphan paths call logger.Printf unconditionally — passing nil logger with orphans causes nil pointer dereference panic. Fix: guard at function top or document non-nil requirement. (2) polecat_spawn.go: SpawnPolecatForSling calls townConfig.ShortInstallationID() without checking for empty InstallationID. Empty ID produces prefix gt- shared across all unconfigured installations — namespace collision risk. restartDaytonaPolecatSession guards against this but spawn does not. Fix: add guard after loading townConfig.
notes: Implemented both guards: (1) Nil logger in Reconcile replaced with io.Discard logger at function entry, removed redundant nil check on ActionHealthy path. (2) Empty InstallationID guard added to SpawnPolecatForSling after loading townConfig, matching the existing pattern in restartDaytonaPolecatSession. Added TestReconcile_NilLoggerDoesNotPanic test. All tests pass.
title: Guard nil logger in Reconcile and empty InstallationID in spawn

## Bug

When multiple `gt sling` commands run concurrently (e.g., slinging 3 beads at once), they all call `FindIdlePolecat()` which has no cross-process locking. All processes get the same idle polecat, then race to:

1. `ReuseIdlePolecat()` — multiple processes reset the same agent bead
2. `AllocateAndAdd()` — fallback allocation may also collide on the same name
3. Daytona `Create()` — "workspace already exists" errors when two processes try to create the same workspace name

### Root Cause

`FindIdlePolecat()` in `manager.go:2589` iterates the polecat list and returns the first idle one with no atomicity. There's no file lock, advisory lock, or CAS operation to prevent concurrent claims.

### Impact

- Parallel slings fail with "Sandbox with name ... already exists"
- Orphaned workspaces created in Daytona but not tracked in local polecat state
- Only 1 of N parallel slings succeeds; rest fail and need manual retry

### Fix Options

1. **File-based lock**: acquire a lockfile (e.g., `<rig>/.polecat-lock`) before FindIdlePolecat + ReuseIdlePolecat
2. **Optimistic CAS in beads**: `ReuseIdlePolecat` atomically sets agent_state from "idle" to "spawning", fails if already claimed
3. **Sequential dispatch**: sling should dispatch polecats one at a time (simplest fix)

### Reproduction

```bash
# Sling 3 beads in parallel to the same rig
gt sling hq-abc GasTownDaytona &
gt sling hq-def GasTownDaytona &
gt sling hq-ghi GasTownDaytona &
wait
```

All three try to claim the same idle polecat.

### Files

- `internal/polecat/manager.go:2589` — `FindIdlePolecat()` (no locking)
- `internal/cmd/polecat_spawn.go:197-300` — reuse path (no atomicity)
notes: Analysis: FindIdlePolecat (manager.go:1836) iterates polecats with no lock. State is derived from agent_state field in beads. ReuseIdlePolecat has per-polecat lock but the race is before it's called. AllocateAndAdd already has proper pool+polecat locking. Fix: Create FindAndClaimIdlePolecat that holds pool lock while finding AND claiming (setting agent_state from idle to spawning). Update polecat_spawn.go to use it instead of separate FindIdlePolecat+ReuseIdlePolecat.
title: Parallel sling race condition: FindIdlePolecat has no cross-process locking

description: RemoveWithOptions (manager.go:1259-1426) has no code to stop or delete the daytona workspace when removing a remote polecat. ## Task

`gt up` should automatically spawn `gt-proxy-server` in a dedicated tmux pane, and `gt down` should stop it cleanly. Currently the proxy must be started/stopped manually.

### Requirements

**gt up:**
- Spawn `gt-proxy-server --town-root <town-root>` in a tmux pane (like witness, refinery, deacon)
- Use a well-known session/window name scoped to the town (e.g., `gt-proxy-<install-short-id>`) so multiple towns can coexist
- Only start if not already running for this town
- Pass the correct `--town-root` so the proxy uses the right CA, config, and repo paths

**gt down:**
- Find and kill the proxy tmux pane belonging to *this* town only
- Do NOT kill proxy servers from other towns (multiple towns may be running simultaneously)
- Match by the town-scoped session/pane name, not by process name globally
- Graceful shutdown: send SIGTERM (the proxy already handles it via signal.NotifyContext)

### Design Notes

- The proxy tmux session name should encode the town identity (e.g., install short ID or town root hash) to avoid collisions
- Consider adding a `--no-proxy` flag to `gt up` for environments where the proxy isn't needed (local-only rigs)
- The proxy pane could live in the existing `gt-<town>` tmux session as a new window, or as a standalone session

### Files to investigate

- `internal/cmd/up.go` — gt up implementation
- `internal/cmd/down.go` — gt down implementation  
- `cmd/gt-proxy-server/main.go` — proxy server entry point
- `internal/tmux/` — tmux session management utilities
title: gt up/down should manage gt-proxy-server lifecycle in tmux

description: Re-injecting certs at each workspace creation via daytona exec base64 trick is fragile and slow. Daytona supports named volumes that persist across sandbox restarts. Fix: create shared cert volume during gt prime or first polecat creation, pass --volume gt-certs-<installID>:/run/gt-proxy in CreateOptions, update volume on cert rotation instead of per-workspace. Affected: internal/polecat/manager.go injectCertsIntoWorkspace().
notes: Implemented: Added --volume flag support to daytona CreateOptions and Client.Create(). Each workspace now mounts a shared cert volume (gt-certs-<installPrefix>) at /run/gt-proxy so certs persist across stop/start cycles. Certs still injected fresh per-polecat since each gets unique client certs.
title: No support for Daytona volumes (persistent cert storage)

description: When loading RigSettings with a RemoteBackend configured, there is no validation that Provider is set or that it equals 'daytona' (the only supported value). validateRigSettings() in loader.go (around line 218) does not check RemoteBackend fields. Validation only happens at usage time in polecat_discover.go:113-114. An invalid provider (e.g. typo 'dyatona') won't be caught until spawn time, leading to confusing errors. Fix: Add validation in validateRigSettings(): if RemoteBackend \!= nil, require Provider \!= '' and Provider == 'daytona'.
notes: Implemented: Added RemoteBackend provider validation in validateRigSettings(). When RemoteBackend is non-nil, Provider must be non-empty and equal 'daytona'. Added ErrInvalidRemoteBackend sentinel error and 3 test cases.
title: No RemoteBackend provider validation at config load time

description: CRITICAL: Manager.SetProxyAdmin() and SessionManager.SetProxyAdmin() exist but are NEVER called anywhere in the codebase. This means m.proxyAdmin is always nil, making denyCertForPolecat() and denyCertOnStop() silently no-op in EVERY removal path (nuke, remove, witness cleanup, session stop). The entire revocation infrastructure (deny list, admin API endpoint, client wrapper, serial storage in beads) exists but was never wired up. No polecat mTLS cert is ever revoked. Fix: Call SetProxyAdmin() in all Manager/SessionManager creation paths — getPolecatManager() in cmd/polecat.go, NewSessionManager usage in cmd/polecat.go, daemon restart paths, witness handlers.
notes: Implemented: Wired SetProxyAdmin() in NewManager() and NewSessionManager() constructors. When RemoteBackend is configured in rig settings, the proxy AdminClient is automatically created and set, enabling cert revocation (denyCertForPolecat/denyCertOnStop) in all removal paths. No caller changes needed — the constructors handle it.
title: SetProxyAdmin() never called — cert revocation completely dead

description: ParseWorkspaceName (client.go:86-101) splits on the LAST hyphen to separate rig from polecat. This means rig names with hyphens work correctly (e.g. 'my-rig') but polecat names with hyphens would be misparsed. For example: workspace 'gt-abc12345-myrig-my-polecat' would parse as rig='myrig-my' polecat='polecat' instead of rig='myrig' polecat='my-polecat'. Current polecat names are single words from the name pool so this is not a bug today, but the constraint is not documented or enforced. Fix: Document in the workspace naming convention that polecat names MUST NOT contain hyphens, or switch to a different delimiter (e.g. double-hyphen '--' between rig and polecat).
notes: Implemented: Changed workspace name format from <prefix>-<rig>-<polecat> to <prefix>-<rig>--<polecat> using double-hyphen delimiter. ParseWorkspaceName now splits on first '--' instead of last '-', allowing both rig and polecat names to contain single hyphens. Added '--' rejection to ValidatePoolName. Updated all affected tests including new test cases for hyphenated polecat names (bullet-farmer, road-warrior).
title: ParseWorkspaceName assumes polecat names contain no hyphens
